classification_rationale: This is a LapSim by SurgicalScience tutorial video with on-screen instructor cues ('Pull back both instruments to start exercise', 'Tie the first knot', 'Pull the knot tighter', 'Tie the second knot', 'Tighten the knot squarely', 'Pull the knot tighter'), narrated guidance, and step-by-step instructional prompts. The video demonstrates the task with pedagogical overlays rather than a trainee performance attempt.
reason_no_score: Instructional/demonstration videos are not scored per protocol. This is educational content showing correct technique, not a trainee performance assessment.

frame_analyses.description: Black screen with LapSim branding
LapSim by SurgicalScience title screen
Instructor avatar with text 'Pull back both instruments to start exercise' — task initialization screen

FLS trainer visible with Penrose drain on suture block, two green marks visible on drain (entry/exit points), two needle drivers positioned bilaterally. Instructor text: 'Tie the first knot.' Task begins.

Right needle driver (left side of screen) grasping suture/needle, left driver (right side) visible. Both instruments approaching the drain. Suture partially visible. Demonstrating initial positioning.
Both drivers manipulating suture in preparation for first knot tying. Suture looped, drivers positioned for wrap initiation.
First wrap of suture being formed between drivers. Right driver leading the wrap motion. Clean, controlled loop formation visible.
Completing first throw of surgeon's knot (double throw). Suture crossing clearly visible, both drivers engaged in knot formation.
Second loop of surgeon's knot being formed. Right driver still leading. Demonstrating proper double-throw technique.
Instructor text: 'Pull the knot tighter.' Drivers tightening completed first throw. Knot cinching cleanly against drain. Green marks visible at closure site.
First throw completed and tightened. Knot appears secure on drain. Instructor text continues guidance.
Instructor text: 'Tie the second knot.' Drivers repositioning for second throw (single throw). Clear hand-switch preparation visible.
Second single throw being initiated. Left driver now leading (switched from right). Single wrap formation visible.
Second throw loop tightening. Knot securing smoothly. Demonstrating clean single-throw technique with hand switch.
Instructor text: 'Tighten the knot squarely.' Second throw fully tightened. Knot appears well-secured.
Instructor text: 'Pull the knot tighter.' Final tightening of second throw. Knot tension optimized.
Repositioning for third throw. Right driver repositioning (switching hand leadership back from left). Third single throw preparation.
Third single throw loop being formed. Right driver leading. Demonstrating final hand-switch requirement.
Third throw tightening. Knot appearing secure with all three throws complete and cinched.
All three throws completed and tightened. Drain slit closure visible, no apparent gap. Knot positioned at marked closure line.
Suture ends visible extending from knot site. Both suture tails present and accessible for cutting.
Final frame showing completed knot and drain configuration. Instruments withdrawn. Task demonstrated as complete.
LapSim branding screen — end of instructional sequence

demonstrated_techniques: Proper surgeon's knot formation (double throw on first knot)
Hand-switching protocol between throws (right leads throw 1, left leads throw 2, right leads throw 3)
Controlled suture wrap initiation and tightening mechanics
Clean knot positioning at the marked closure site
Bilateral driver coordination for intracorporeal knot construction
Sequential single-throw construction after initial surgeon's knot
verbal_cues: Pull back both instruments to start exercise (initialization cue)
Tie the first knot (surgeon's knot instruction)
Tie the second knot (second throw instruction)
Pull the knot tighter (final tightening emphasis)
common_errors_addressed: Incomplete double throw on first knot — demo shows full two-loop formation
Failure to switch hand leadership between throws — demo explicitly alternates
Slack knots or loose cinching — demo emphasizes progressive tightening
Knot positioned off the marked line — demo centers knot at green mark indicators
Excessive tension causing drain deformation — demo uses controlled, moderate pull tension
expert_indicators: Smooth, efficient instrument coordination with minimal wasted motion
Clean, symmetrical knot loops with proper crossing geometry
Consistent hand-switching between throws without hesitation
Appropriate knot tension balancing security and tissue preservation
Completion in approximately 72 seconds (well within expert benchmark of 80–120s)

observational_summary: This LapSim instructional video provides a step-by-step demonstration of correct intracorporeal suture placement and knot-tying technique for FLS task 5. The video shows proper surgeon's knot formation (double throw) followed by two single throws with appropriate hand-switching at each phase. Knot cinching is demonstrated with controlled tension, and the final knot is positioned cleanly at the marked closure site. The video serves as a reference standard for trainee learning but is not itself a trainee performance and therefore not scored.
